title: too many levels of symbolic links (again)
body:
### Issue Description

I get a `too many levels of symbolic links` error trying to run a specific container.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. git clone https://github.com/openshift/release
2. cd release
3. export CONTAINER_ENGINE=podman
4. make jobs


### Describe the results you received

```shell
$ make jobs
false || podman pull --platform linux/amd64 registry.ci.openshift.org/ci/ci-operator-checkconfig:latest
Trying to pull registry.ci.openshift.org/ci/ci-operator-checkconfig:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:9f1439242edb0a4cc68f5393257826c7f7cbef59d638e6b85aa37409de4d7f6c
Copying blob sha256:a90863a9fe720b22846ec4bbc2fba697a2a7a924da0f17801fb51dd485453dd5
Copying blob sha256:721c5f2b9838fecaacf49e5b59dd36278a9d0e53622abb62913bc8656e6e8889
Copying blob sha256:58abf6b9519416ae6f05a2f8ce82b0790d46badcb394b3b9e9b3183326d1391c
Copying config sha256:4c3ab32b07c376b6cf9c009536b29c883ce2cede9ca11cc1ee55be32690a2180
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
4c3ab32b07c376b6cf9c009536b29c883ce2cede9ca11cc1ee55be32690a2180
podman run  --platform linux/amd64 --rm -v "/Users/angoldst/code/openshift-release/ci-operator/config:/ci-operator/config" -v "/Users/angoldst/code/openshift-release/ci-operator/step-registry:/ci-operator/step-registry" registry.ci.openshift.or
g/ci/ci-operator-checkconfig:latest --config-dir /ci-operator/config --registry /ci-operator/step-registry
time="2023-02-16T15:41:52Z" level=fatal msg="failed to parse arguments" error="failed to load registry: open /ci-operator/step-registry/ipi/install/install/stableinitial/ipi-install-install-stableinitial-commands.sh: too many levels of symbolic
 links"
make: *** [ci-operator-checkconfig] Error 1
```

### Describe the results you expected

No error

### podman info output

### Additional information

comments.body:
I've tried to reproduce locally on a Fedora 37 machine without any luck, so it might be related to podman remote.

Just to make sure, is the container expected to exit without any output?
I don't recall offhand. It might have no output.

DEBU[0090] Failed to mount container "f850e563ba5b5d29593c5b831fc12180d01c82c820af37858a3b6a9110a1a975": creating overlay mount to /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/618fca21ac65731d1d563d5a19920aae3cf0d7591cb3d6597bb533dac1179dfe/merged, mount_data="lowerdir=/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/KJ7MNRBDJF35HLR5YGM4MZXGX6:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/KJ7MNRBDJF35HLR5YGM4MZXGX6/../diff1:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/KXCQ2MKEBN7S5EHWSJSVJJ3CEE:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/KXCQ2MKEBN7S5EHWSJSVJJ3CEE/../diff1:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/YITIBFUWSUS5TPAA6OOAD7SRKV:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/YITIBFUWSUS5TPAA6OOAD7SRKV/../diff1:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/2SCY5W4MHO2G22CUDGXEVLZ73L:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/2SCY5W4MHO2G22CUDGXEVLZ73L/../diff1:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/GIXZ4YKRXKJAMKVZLKAYI27SMI:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/GIXZ4YKRXKJAMKVZLKAYI27SMI/../diff1:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/ZTYM2SPJ4SALHWUCKRJUCX6VED:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/ZTYM2SPJ4SALHWUCKRJUCX6VED/../diff1:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/D4TNYWAEL22YOVZBVS7YYVUSMY:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/D4TNYWAEL22YOVZBVS7YYVUSMY/../diff1,upperdir=/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/618fca21ac65731d1d563d5a19920aae3cf0d7591cb3d6597bb533dac1179dfe/diff,workdir=/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/618fca21ac65731d1d563d5a19920aae3cf0d7591cb3d6597bb533dac1179dfe/work,nodev": too many levels of symbolic links


why there is a directory named `diff1` ?
@fatelei please provide more information.  What command have you used to cause the error?  What version of Podman?

> @fatelei please provide more information. What command have you used to cause the error? What version of Podman?

podman version 4.3.1
@giuseppe why there is a `diff1` name directory ?

it is used to create a copy of the image using overlay metacopy, if you want more details you can find them in the c/storage commit: https://github.com/containers/storage/commit/957831cf61c742a247dc9fd45902490c27738eed
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@ncdc are you still seeing this issue, we need a repeater.
Yes. Happy to provide as much debugging info as you need. Just let me know how I can help. Thanks!
@rhatdan do you have folks who can help investigate? I'm not able to use podman for 100% of my local containerization needs b/c of this 😢

Hmm, just retested and this seems to be working now. I'll go ahead and close it.
